prompt: I just created a brand new empty Obsidian vault at /tmp/test-vault/. I want you to set it up with a good folder structure and create a few starter notes — an index note, a daily note template, and a meeting notes template. I'm not sure what organization system to use, just pick something good for a software engineer who takes lots of meeting notes and tracks projects.
assertions: Presents organization system options to the user (mentions PARA, MOC, flat+tags, or Johnny Decimal)
Recommends PARA as the default for the described use case
Creates folder structure with at minimum: Projects, Areas/Resources, Archive, templates, daily-notes, and attachments directories
Creates an index/home note with wikilinks ([[...]]) linking to key areas
Creates a daily note template with YAML frontmatter (--- fenced) including at least tags and date fields
Creates a meeting notes template with YAML frontmatter including at least tags, date, and attendees fields
All internal links use wikilink syntax [[...]] not markdown link syntax []()
Templates use proper Obsidian template variables like {{date}} or {{title}}
